articles.summary: The National Stock Exchange (NSE) is likely to target a valuation of ~6-7 trillion ($65-75 billion) for its upcoming IPO. At the higher end, it would rank among India's most valuable listed companies. The IPO could be India's largest, surpassing Hyundai Motor's offering.

articles.headline: War scars begin to show on India Inc
articles.summary: The Iran war's economic impact is showing on India Inc, with export disruptions, supply-chain snags, and gas supply tightening affecting sectors. Automakers and component manufacturers worry about fuel availability for industrial processes.

articles.headline: JV emerges L1 bidder for ~274 crore MRTH order
articles.summary: A joint venture has emerged as the lowest (L1) bidder for a ~274 crore order from Mumbai Railway Vikas Corporation (MRTH).

articles.headline: Bajel Projects awarded significant order from Tata Power
articles.summary: Bajel Projects has secured a significant order from Tata Power for transmission lines, valued at ~2,175 crore.

articles.headline: Glenmark Pharma to launch Potassium Phosphates injection in US
articles.summary: Glenmark Pharmaceuticals will launch its Potassium Phosphates injection in the US, leveraging its USP.

articles.headline: Lemon Tree bets long on W Asia despite conflict
articles.summary: Lemon Tree Hotels plans to expand in West Asia despite the Iran-Israel-US conflict, anticipating new opportunities post-resolution. They also plan global expansion, including Southeast Asia.

articles.headline: ReNew's C&I arm secures $95 million equity funding
articles.summary: ReNew Green Energy Solutions' C&I arm secured $95 million in equity investment from LeapFrog Investments and Carlyle AlpInvest for expansion.

articles.headline: Vedanta committee approves raising ~2,575 crore via NCDs
articles.summary: Vedanta's directors committee approved raising up to ~2,575 crore through non-convertible debentures to diversify funding and strengthen its balance sheet.

articles.headline: India can emerge as strategic hub for unmanned systems: GAGC chief
articles.summary: GAGC CEO Vivek Lall believes India can be a global hub for unmanned systems manufacturing due to its talent, industry, and policy environment, particularly for surveillance drones.

articles.headline: MakeMyTrip weighs India IPO as part of long-term plan
articles.summary: MakeMyTrip is considering listing on Indian stock exchanges, seeing it as a catalyst for brand growth and capital access in its core market.

articles.headline: PhonePe defers IPO amid global headwinds
articles.summary: PhonePe has temporarily deferred its India IPO due to West Asia conflict and market volatility, aiming to resume listing once global markets stabilize.

articles.headline: No port pileup as exports to West Asia continue
articles.summary: Despite West Asia crisis, container movement to the region continues smoothly with no port congestion. Around 2,600 containers shipped in five days.

articles.headline: CGD companies offer free gas, fee waivers on ECB plans
articles.summary: City gas distribution firms are offering incentives like free gas and fee waivers to promote domestic PNG connections amid LPG shortages.

articles.headline: NBFCs hold back on ECB plans as hedging cost rises
articles.summary: NBFCs are holding back external commercial borrowing plans due to rising hedging costs and interest rates caused by geopolitical tensions.

articles.headline: DPIIT notifies changes in FDI norms for China
articles.summary: DPIIT has eased FDI norms for China and other land border countries, allowing up to 10% non-controlling stakes under the automatic route.

articles.headline: Dubai halts flights at main airport after fuel tank attack
articles.summary: Dubai temporarily halted flights at its main airport after a drone strike on a nearby fuel tank, affecting operations amid the Iran conflict.

articles.headline: Foxconn forecasts strong revenue growth for 2026
articles.summary: Foxconn expects strong revenue growth in Q1 and full-year 2026, driven by AI servers, despite a 2% drop in quarterly profit.

articles.headline: Cost asymmetry: War creates opportunity for Indian firms
articles.summary: The shift in warfare dynamics, with cheaper drones challenging expensive Western weapons, presents opportunities for India's indigenous defence sector and startups.

articles.headline: Sensex, Nifty get a breather; broader markets weak
articles.summary: Benchmark indices rose over 1% but broader markets remained weak, with declining stocks outnumbering advancers amid rising crude oil prices and geopolitical concerns.

articles.headline: Nomura, Citi slash year-end Nifty 50 target
articles.summary: Foreign brokerages Nomura and Citi have cut their year-end Nifty 50 targets due to ongoing West Asia conflict and potential impact on earnings.

articles.headline: ICICI Bank seen riding out macro headwinds
articles.summary: ICICI Bank's resilient credit growth and healthy asset quality position it well against macro headwinds, with analysts maintaining a 'buy' rating.

articles.headline: Bandhan Bank slides 7.5% on reports of promoter exit options
articles.summary: Bandhan Bank shares fell as its promoter explored options like stake sale or IPO to facilitate investor exits.

articles.headline: Fino Payments Bank shares dip 19% on reports of ED probe
articles.summary: Fino Payments Bank shares dropped amid reports suggesting a potential probe by the Enforcement Directorate into certain online gaming transactions.

articles.headline: Sebi outlines measures to boost investment advisory ecosystem
articles.summary: Sebi is forming a working group to review regulations for MFDs and IAs, launch a digital platform for guidance, and introduce a common advertisement code.

articles.headline: Centre discusses rare-earth corridor sites in Andhra, Odisha, Kerala
articles.summary: The government discussed potential sites for rare-earth corridors in four states, focusing on beach sand minerals and regulatory hurdles like monazite handling.

articles.headline: IndiGo to face pressure from fuel price spike: Moody’s
articles.summary: Moody's reports IndiGo will face profitability pressure from rising fuel prices, though its short booking curve may allow cost pass-through.

articles.headline: The great Indian bank CEO shortage
articles.summary: Private sector banks face a CEO talent shortage due to strict compensation norms and compensation mismatch with other financial sectors, impacting succession planning.

articles.headline: Hard Rock Café winds down its India gig
articles.summary: Hard Rock International terminates its franchise agreement with JSM Corporation, leading to the closure of 10 Hard Rock Café outlets across India.

articles.headline: NMDC scripts history as India's first mining company to reach 50 MT
articles.summary: NMDC has become India's first mining company to produce 50 million tonnes of iron ore in a financial year, a fivefold increase since 1978.

articles.headline: Union Bank settles term insurance of Rs 20L
articles.summary: Union Bank settled a Rs 20 lakh term insurance claim for the family of a deceased OMCL employee under its customized salary package.

articles.headline: CBSE warns against misleading info on exam evaluation
articles.summary: The CBSE has warned of legal action against individuals posting 'factually incorrect information' about its examination and evaluation processes on social media. The board also cautioned teachers that disciplinary action could be taken for sharing comments on the confidential evaluation process. It emphasized that strict protocols govern the secrecy of the results process.

articles.headline: IIT Kharagpur to upskill workforce in AI
articles.summary: IIT Kharagpur is partnering with LTIMindtree to upskill the workforce in Artificial Intelligence (AI).

articles.headline: Queen Elizabeth’s Global Schools turns first page of India chapter
articles.summary: Queen Elizabeth’s Global Schools will open its first Indian campus in Gurugram in August 2026, with plans for more campuses and cautious use of AI.

articles.headline: Population scientist Paul R. Ehrlich dies at age 93
articles.summary: Paul R. Ehrlich, the renowned author of the controversial best-seller 'The Population Bomb', has died in California. His 1968 book predicted global famine and transformed him into a leader of the environmental movement. While some of his scarcity forecasts were criticized as premature, the book sold three million copies and fundamentally shaped debates on global resources.

articles.headline: Indian Military Endorses New AI Policy for Future Autonomous Warfare
articles.summary: India’s triservices AI policy has formally endorsed the development of Lethal Autonomous Weapon Systems (LAWS), drone swarms, and AI-driven cyberattacks. The policy emphasizes 'meaningful human oversight' while pitching for deep industry collaboration. It includes plans for a dedicated defence cloud and AI-enabled predictive analytics to gain an edge in modern battlefields.

articles.headline: Why Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli is worth the Formula 1 hype
articles.summary: Italian teenager Andrea 'Kimi' Antonelli has silenced skeptics with a historic debut season for Mercedes. At 18, he became the second-youngest winner and youngest pole-sitter in F1 history. Skipping F3 to move directly to F2, Antonelli has displayed exceptional aggressive driving and sensitivity. His performance in the 2025 season has already established him as a title contender.

articles.headline: Cameron Young wins The Players Championship at TPC Sawgrass
articles.summary: Cameron Young secured his biggest career title by winning The Players Championship by one shot. Young produced a crucial birdie on the 17th island green and a massive 375-yard drive on the final hole to finish at 13-under 275. He beat Matt Fitzpatrick, while leader Ludvig Aberg imploded on the back nine. Young earned $4.5 million and moved to world No. 4.

articles.headline: AFC confirms Iran set to participate in World Cup
articles.summary: Asian Football Confederation General Secretary Windsor John stated that Iran's national team is still on track to play in the World Cup in North America. This follows social media posts by the Iranian federation asserting they cannot be excluded. The team is scheduled to play group matches in California and Seattle, despite ongoing regional conflict and safety concerns raised by the US.

articles.headline: Sanju Samson trade brings renewed optimism to CSK camp
articles.summary: Chennai Super Kings are banking on Kerala star Sanju Samson to succeed MS Dhoni as the franchise's main crowd-puller. Samson joined CSK in a high-profile trade involving Ravindra Jadeja. Following a successful T20 World Cup campaign, Samson is expected to lead a younger, more aggressive CSK lineup alongside captain Ruturaj Gaikwad as the team looks to recover from a last-place finish.

articles.headline: M Chinnaswamy Stadium approved to host IPL matches
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has granted formal approval to host IPL 2026 matches at Bengaluru's M Chinnaswamy Stadium. Approval followed safety and security evaluations by an expert committee. The stadium will host the tournament opener between Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Sunrisers Hyderabad on March 28. RCB will play five of its seven home games at the venue.

articles.headline: Dhakshineswar Suresh ready for professional tennis transition
articles.summary: Davis Cup star Dhakshineswar Suresh will graduate from Wake Forest University in May and turn professional. The 25-year-old from Madurai has delivered three consecutive Davis Cup wins for India, including a recent victory over the Netherlands. Experts suggest his commitment to both singles and doubles, combined with his 6-foot-5 frame and powerful serve, makes him a promising pro prospect.

articles.headline: Jannik Sinner defeats Medvedev for first Indian Wells title
articles.summary: World number two Jannik Sinner captured his first Indian Wells ATP Masters 1000 crown by beating Daniil Medvedev in straight sets. Sinner rallied from a 0-4 deficit in the second-set tiebreaker to secure the victory. He became one of the few players to win all six hard-court Masters 1000 tournaments, maintaining his unbeaten set streak throughout the tournament.

articles.headline: North Zone wins JK Bose T20 cricket trophy
articles.summary: North Zone secured the JK Bose T20 title after a dominant victory over East Zone in New Delhi. Chasing a target of 136, North Zone reached the mark in just 9.5 overs, led by Rishabh Singh’s unbeaten 87 off 40 balls. In other events, Shahid Judge won the Table Tennis title representing SJAM-1. The tournament concluded at the Roshanara Cricket Ground.

articles.headline: Court disqualifies Kiran More from Baroda cricket polls
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court ruled that former India wicketkeeper Kiran More and three others are ineligible to contest the Baroda Cricket Association elections. The court cited a violation of tenure norms established by the Supreme Court. The verdict followed a legal challenge regarding the candidates' eligibility under the current administrative framework.

articles.headline: Middle East Conflict Causes Widespread Cancellations of Global Sporting Events
articles.summary: All top-flight football matches in Iran, Qatar, and Bahrain have been indefinitely postponed due to regional safety concerns. The 'Finalissima' match between Argentina and Spain in Qatar has also been cancelled. Other impacted events include Formula One races in Bahrain and Saudi Arabia, as well as several ATP tennis events in the UAE following drone scares.

articles.headline: ECI Transfers Top Bengal Officials and Cops Ahead of Polls
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India has ordered the transfer of West Bengal's DGP Peeyush Pandey, Kolkata Police Commissioner, Chief Secretary, and Home Secretary, appointing Dushyant Nariala as new Chief Secretary. The move, aimed at ensuring administrative neutrality for the April polls, sparked a political row.

articles.headline: BJP Fields Rajeev Chandrasekhar from Nemom in Kerala Assembly List
articles.summary: The BJP released its first list of 47 candidates for the Kerala Assembly polls, fielding Union Minister Rajeev Chandrasekhar from Nemom, its sole seat in the state. The list includes seven women and six SC members, with elections set for April 9.

articles.headline: Karnataka Speaker Adjourns House Over Lack of Government Answers
articles.summary: Karnataka Assembly Speaker UT Khader adjourned the session in protest after the government failed to provide written answers to unstarred questions. Home Minister G Parameshwara assured that such lapses would not recur.

articles.headline: India, US Set to Clash on WTO Reforms
articles.summary: India and the US are set for a clash at the WTO over reforms. India insists on maintaining consensus-based decision-making and MFN treatment, demanding pending mandates be addressed first.

articles.headline: SEBI to Harmonise Roles for Mutual Fund Distributors and Advisers
articles.summary: SEBI is reviewing regulations for mutual fund distributors and investment advisers to clarify roles. A common advertisement code and a digital compliance platform, SEBI SETU, are also being developed.

articles.headline: India's Wholesale Inflation Rises to 11-Month High of 2.13% in February
articles.summary: India's wholesale inflation rose to 2.13% in February, driven by food and mineral prices. Manufacturing inflation also increased. The surge in crude oil prices due to the West Asia conflict is expected to impact prices further.

articles.headline: India’s Trade Deficit Doubles to $27 Billion in February
articles.summary: India's merchandise trade deficit widened to $27 billion in February as imports surged 24%, driven by a 218.5% rise in gold imports. Exports dipped slightly, impacted by reduced petroleum shipments. The gap with China reached $102 billion.

articles.headline: India-US Trade Deal Delayed Over Finalization of New Tariff Framework
articles.summary: India-US trade talks are paused pending Washington's new global tariff framework after a Supreme Court ruling. Discussions continue on non-tariff barriers and Section 232 tariffs.

articles.headline: RBI to Conduct ~1.5 Lakh Crore 7-Day Variable Rate Repo Auction
articles.summary: The RBI will conduct a seven-day variable rate repo auction of ₹1.5 lakh crore on March 17 to manage liquidity in the banking system.

articles.headline: 'One Battle After Another' Dominates 98th Academy Awards
articles.summary: 'One Battle After Another' won six Oscars, including Best Picture and Director. Michael B. Jordan and Jessie Buckley won Best Actor and Actress respectively. The ceremony saw political statements amidst tightened security.

articles.headline: PhonePe Pauses IPO Plans Citing Market Volatility
articles.summary: PhonePe has temporarily halted its IPO plans due to geopolitical tensions and market volatility, aiming for a $10.5 billion valuation upon listing when stability returns.

articles.headline: Reliance Signs $3 Billion Green Ammonia Supply Deal with Samsung C&T
articles.summary: Reliance Industries signed a 15-year, $3 billion deal with Samsung C&T to supply green ammonia from H2 FY29, positioning India as a major green energy exporter.

articles.headline: Ola Electric Plans Rs 2,000 Crore Stake Sale in Battery Unit
articles.summary: Ola Electric aims to raise up to ₹2,000 crore by selling a stake in its battery subsidiary, Ola Cell Technologies, to fund its cell manufacturing plant expansion.

articles.headline: Union Bank Board Approves Issuance of Long-Term Bonds
articles.summary: Union Bank of India's board approved issuing up to ₹20,000 crore in long-term bonds to finance housing and infrastructure, including ₹5,000 crore in Green/Sustainable Bonds.

articles.headline: FSSAI Grants Authorisation to 17 r-PET Plants
articles.summary: FSSAI authorized 17 recycled PET plants for food-contact packaging, aiding India's 2025-26 recycled content target and strengthening the supply chain.

articles.headline: IDBI Bank Shares Plunge on Potential Divestment Deferment
articles.summary: IDBI Bank shares dropped 16.5% amid reports the government might defer stake sale due to low bids. The bank stated no official confirmation of the plan being scrapped.
